title: SWOT Analysis Framework
description: A classic corporate diagnostic framework evaluating a company's internal strengths and weaknesses alongside external opportunities and threats.

quadrants.name: Strengths (Internal)
quadrants.metrics: High operating profit margin, strong brand equity, patented technology assets, low merchant attrition rates.
quadrants.action: Consolidate and leverage these competitive advantages to cross-sell additional strategic services.

quadrants.name: Weaknesses (Internal)
quadrants.metrics: High operational delay periods, excessive administrative overhead, unoptimized debt structure, low current liquidity.
quadrants.action: Implement zero-based budgets, streamline logistical supply chains, and reduce expensive short-term leverage.

quadrants.name: Opportunities (External)
quadrants.metrics: New geographic market niches, emerging predictive automation, changing price elasticities, scaling online portals.
quadrants.action: Allocate core resources and capture rapid growth loops before competitors establish presence.

quadrants.name: Threats (External)
quadrants.metrics: Aggressive competitor price cuts, credit market contractions, rising logistics disruptions, sudden compliance revisions.
quadrants.action: Hedge market risks, draft strict SLA contracts, and establish contingency capital credit lines.
